classifications.name: Wary Sentries
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Perception checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Resurgence
classifications.description: When you catch your Second Wind, you immediately gain a Move Action to be used immediately.

classifications.name: Spray Shot
classifications.description: When you have a weapon set on Autofire, you can reduce the area targeted by your Autofire attack to 1 square.

classifications.name: Improved Charge
classifications.description: You can make a Charge without having to move in a straight line, and you can alter your direction when making a Charge to avoid obstacles. All other Charge rules apply.

classifications.name: Battering Attack
classifications.description: Whenever you successfully move a creature with the Bantha Rush Feat, you also knock that target Prone.

classifications.name: Unwavering Focus
classifications.description: Impose a penalty to Skill Checks for Mind-Affecting effects that target you.

classifications.name: Expert Droid Repair
classifications.description: You can use Repair Droid to a number of Droids equal to your Intelligence modifier (Minimum 2) simultaneously. You make Mechanics checks for each Droid as normal.

classifications.name: Forceful Strike
classifications.description: You may spend a Force Point to move a target -1 step down the condition track when using Force Stun.

classifications.name: Galactic Alliance Military Training
classifications.description: You do not move down the Condition Track the first time an attack exceeds your Damage Threshold in an encounter.

classifications.name: Increased Resistance
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to Fortitude Defense when an enemy fails to affect you.

classifications.name: Slippery Maneuver
classifications.description: You can apply the effects of the Dodge Feat against attacks made from two opponents. In addition, when you use the Withdraw Action to move away from a target against whom you use the Dodge Feat, you can move at your full Speed. You still provoke an Attack of Opportunity if you must move more than 1 square to escape a Threatened Area.

classifications.name: Primitive Warrior
classifications.description: Deal +1 die of damage with Simple Weapons (Melee).

classifications.name: Flurry
classifications.description: When wielding only Light weapons or Lightsabers, you can make a flurry of attacks. You take a -5 penalty to your Reflex Defense, but you gain a +2 bonus on your melee attack rolls. This bonus on attacks and penalty to Reflex Defense apply until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Point-Blank Shot
classifications.description: You get a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with ranged weapons against opponents within Point-Blank Range.

classifications.name: Low Profile
classifications.description: You gain cover from enemies at long range and may reroll Stealth checks made to avoid detection.

classifications.name: Fast Surge
classifications.description: On your turn, you can catch a Second Wind as a Free Action instead of a Swift Action.

classifications.name: Wounding Strike
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you may move a target -1 step down the condition track when you hit with a melee attack.

classifications.name: Pitiless Warrior
classifications.description: Gain Bonus Hit Points whenever you take down a foe.

classifications.name: Scion of Dorin
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to Fortitude Defense against Atmospheric Hazards.

classifications.name: Saber Throw
classifications.description: You may throw your lightsaber as a ranged attack using Use the Force instead of a ranged attack roll. The lightsaber returns to your hand at the end of the turn.

classifications.name: Pincer
classifications.description: When you successfully Pin an enemy, you can maintain the Pin beyond 1 round and can make subsequent Grapple checks against the Pinned enemy as a Swift Action. You can apply the Crush Feat whenever your subsequent checks are successful.

classifications.name: Grapple Resistance
classifications.description: You get a +5 bonus to your Reflex Defense when an enemy makes a Grab or Grapple attack against you, and you get a +5 bonus to all opposed Grapple checks. All objects that you are holding or carrying gain a +5 bonus to their Reflex Defense when attacked (see Attack an Object).

classifications.name: Unstoppable Force
classifications.description: You gain a +5 insight bonus to Fortitude Defense and Will Defense against any attack or effect requiring a Use the Force check.

classifications.name: Conditioning
classifications.description:
You can reroll any
Strength
- or Constitution -based Skill Checks for Skills that you are Trained in. The result of the reroll must be accepted even if it is worse. Additionally, once per encounter you can add your Strength modifier to your Fortitude Defense as a Reaction ; this bonus lasts until the beginning of your next turn.

classifications.name: Stealthy
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Stealth checks and may reroll a failed Stealth check once per encounter.

classifications.name: Burst Fire
classifications.description:
When using a ranged weapon with Autofire capability, and that you are Proficient with, in Autofire mode, you may fire a short burst as a single attack against a single target. You take a -5 penalty on your attack roll, but deal +2 dice of damage.

The effects of this Feat do not stack with the extra damage provided by the Deadeye or Rapid Shot feats.

If you do not have a Strength of 13 or higher, increase the penalty on attacks to -10 when using this Feat with non- Vehicle Weapons.

classifications.name: Zero Range
classifications.description: When firing a ranged weapon at a target within or adjacent to your Fighting Space, you gain a +1 bonus on the attack roll and deal +1 die of damage on a hit.

classifications.name: Mounted Regiment
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Ride checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Forceful Throw
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Move Object.

classifications.name: Toughness
classifications.description: You gain +1 Hit Point per Character Level.

classifications.name: Grazing Shot
classifications.description: When you successfully make a ranged attack against a single target, you can make a second attack roll against an additional target that is in direct line of sight, and no farther than 6 squares from the original target. If the second attack roll succeeds, make a single damage roll, and divide the damage equally between the two targets. If the second attack roll fails, you deal no damage to either target.

classifications.name: Ascension Specialists
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Climb checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Rapid Reaction
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you can use two different Reactions in response to the same trigger. For example, if you use the Deflect Talent and fail to negate the attack, you can also use Negate Energy as a response to the same attack.

classifications.name: Confident Success
classifications.description: Gain a Force Point when you successfully Learn Secret Information.

classifications.name: Channel Rage
classifications.description: Once per day, instead of entering into a Rage, you gain a +5 bonus to your Will Defense until the end of the encounter. This bonus counts as using your Rage ability for that day.

classifications.name: Skill Focus
classifications.description: Choose one Skill in which you are Trained. You gain a +5 competence bonus on all Skill Checks made with that Skill.

classifications.name: Accelerated Strike
classifications.description: Once per encounter, when using only weapons with which you are proficient, you can take a Full Attack Action as a Standard Action.

classifications.name: Wilderness First Aid
classifications.description: Once per day, you can make a DC 20 Survival check. If the check is successful, when you use the Basic Survival application of the Survival skill, you are considered to have a Medpac for any Treat Injury checks made until the end of the day.

classifications.name: Forceful Will
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Will Defense against mind-affecting effects and may reroll a failed Will Defense check once per encounter.

classifications.name: Reactive Awareness
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you may reroll a Perception check and take the better result.

classifications.name: Technical Experts
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Mechanics checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Logic Upgrade: Self-Defense
classifications.description: Grant yourself +2 to the Defense of your choice for 1 round.

classifications.name: Staggering Attack
classifications.description: When using a melee weapon with which you are proficient, you can make an attack that disrupts an enemy's concentration. You take a -2 penalty on your attack roll, and if your attack deals damage, the target takes a -2 penalty to Skill Checks until the end of your next turn. Alternatively, you can take a -5 penalty to your attack roll, and if your attack deals damage, the target takes a -5 penalty to Skill Checks until end of your next turn.

classifications.name: Strong Bellow
classifications.description: Use your Bellow ability without moving down the Condition Track.

classifications.name: Wrruushi Training
classifications.description: Gain bonus hit points on Unarmed attacks. Deny target Equipment bonus.

classifications.name: Medical Team
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Treat Injury checks when near allies.

classifications.name: K'tara Training
classifications.description: Deal extra damage against Flat-Footed enemy. Render target Mute.

classifications.name: Force Regimen Mastery
classifications.description: You learn a number of Force Regimens equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

classifications.name: Tumble Defense
classifications.description: When an opponent attempts to Tumble through a square you threaten with a melee weapon with which you are proficient, add your Base Attack Bonus to the DC of the Acrobatics check made to Tumble. If the target fails the Acrobatics check to Tumble, you can make an Attack of Opportunity against that opponent as normal.

classifications.name: Exotic Weapon Proficiency
classifications.description: Select a single weapon from either the Exotic Weapons (Melee) or Exotic Weapons (Ranged) weapon groups; you make attack rolls with the selected weapon without penalty.

classifications.name: Close Combat Escape
classifications.description: When you successfully use Acrobatics to escape a Grapple, you can spend a Swift Action to make a single attack with a melee weapon or an Unarmed attack against the opponent that had Grappled you. If the attack hits, it deals normal damage, and your opponent is considered Flat-Footed until the start of its next turn.

classifications.name: Vehicular Combat
classifications.description: Once per round (as a Reaction), when you are piloting a Vehicle or Starship, you may negate a weapon hit by making a successful Pilot check. The DC of the Skill Check is equal to the result of the attack roll you wish to negate. In addition, while you are piloting a Vehicle, you are considered proficient with pilot-operated Vehicle Weapons.

classifications.name: Damage Conversion
classifications.description: When you take damage from an attack against you (other than an Area Attack, or an attack that deals Ion or Force damage) and that damage equals or exceeds your Damage Threshold, you can take an additional 10 points of damage instead of moving down the Condition Track. Each subsequent time you use this Feat in a single encounter, the additional damage you take increases by 5 points.

classifications.name: Lightning Draw
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you can draw a holstered weapon and attack as a single Standard Action.

classifications.name: Tool Frenzy
classifications.description: As a Standard Action, using Appendages not normally considered Weapons, you can make several strikes as a single attack against a single target. Make an Unarmed melee attack with a +2 bonus; until the end of your next turn, you take a -2 penalty to your Reflex Defense. Use the damage die of the highest rated Appendage when determining damage.

classifications.name: Quick Comeback
classifications.description: Recover quickly after being moved down the Condition Track by damage.

classifications.name: Ample Foraging
classifications.description: Grant a bonus to allies' Fortitude Defense by foraging.

classifications.name: Deft Charge
classifications.description: After you Charge, you can take Swift Actions, Reactions, and Free Actions before your turn ends.

classifications.name: Riflemaster
classifications.description:
When you make a ranged attack with a Rifle with which you are proficient, you gain one of the following benefits, depending on the kind of Rifle you use.

- Blaster Carbine: You can Brace a Blaster Carbine set to Autofire, even though it is not an Autofire -only weapon.
- Blaster Rifle: You treat the Blaster Rifle as an Accurate Weapon.
- Heavy Blaster Rifle: When you use a Heavy Blaster Rifle, the damage dice increase from d10 to d12.
- Light Repeating Blaster: You treat the Light Repeating Blaster as if it were Medium instead of Large.

classifications.name: Sharp Senses
classifications.description: Increase the die type when adding Force Points to a Perception check.

classifications.name: Weapon Finesse
classifications.description: When using a Light Melee Weapon or a Lightsaber, you may use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls.

classifications.name: Predictive Defense
classifications.description: You can use either your Dexterity modifier or your Intelligence modifier to determine your Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Disturbing Presence
classifications.description: If you succeed on a DC 15 Deception check, you can move through the Threatened area or Fighting Space of an enemy as part of your move action without provoking an attack of opportunity. Each Threatened space or occupied space that you move through in this manner counts as 2 squares of movement.

classifications.name: Adaptable Talent
classifications.description: Choose one Talent that you meet the prerequisites for, from a Class you possess. Once per day, after at least 6 hours of rest, you can choose to swap out one of your current Talents for the Talent you chose for this Feat. The Talent you swap out cannot be the prerequisite for any other Talent you have. You can swap back to your original Talent after at least 6 hours of rest.

classifications.name: Poison Resistance
classifications.description: You gain a +5 bonus to your Fortitude Defense when attacked by Poison. If the attack succeeds, you take only half damage.

classifications.name: Dive for Cover
classifications.description: Once per turn, as a Reaction to being targeted by a ranged attack, you can make a Jump check to Long Jump. If you land in a square that provides you with Cover from your attacker, you gain the normal Cover bonus against the attack, even if you didn't have Cover at the time the attack was made. When you use this Feat, you always land Prone.

classifications.name: Advantageous Cover
classifications.description: When you have Cover, you take no damage from Area Attacks, even if the attack roll exceeds your Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Fortifying Recovery
classifications.description: When you take the Recover Action, you also gain a number of Bonus Hit Points equal to 2 x your Constitution bonus (Minimum 2). Damage is subtracted from the Bonus Hit Points first, and any Bonus Hit Points remaining at the end of encounter are lost. Bonus Hit Points do not stack.

classifications.name: Flèche
classifications.description: When Charging, turn any natural attack roll of 17+ into a Critical Hit.

classifications.name: Tactical Advantage
classifications.description: When you successfully damage an opponent using an Attack of Opportunity, you can immediately move 1 square in any direction. This movement does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.

classifications.name: Hideous Visage
classifications.description: Once per encounter, as a Swift Action, you can make a Deception check against one opponent that can see you. If the check equals or exceeds the opponent's Will Defense, you move the target 1 square away from you and it takes a -1 penalty on all attacks until the start of your next turn. This is a Mind-Affecting Fear effect.

classifications.name: Improved Stun
classifications.description: You gain a +5 bonus on attack rolls made to stun an opponent.

classifications.name: Cut the Red Tape
classifications.description: You can use your Knowledge (Bureaucracy) modifier instead of your Gather Information modifier when making Gather Information checks. If you are entitled to a Gather Information check reroll, you can reroll your Knowledge (Bureaucracy) check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations). You are considered Trained in the Gather Information skill for the purposes of this check.

classifications.name: Republic Military Training
classifications.description: Once per encounter, as a Reaction, you gain Damage Reduction 10 against an incoming attack if you have Cover from the attacker. You can gain the Damage Reduction as long as you have Cover, even if your opponent uses Aim to ignore your Cover bonus to Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Skill Challenge: Catastrophic Avoidance
classifications.description: Catastrophic Failures in a Skill Challenge occur less frequently, and with milder results.

classifications.name: Weapon Proficiency (Simple Weapons)
classifications.description: You are proficient with simple weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

classifications.name: Impersonate
classifications.description: You can make a Deception check to alter your features to that of a specific person. This Feat also allows you to change your voice to match the target's. You always treat impersonating a person as a Moderate Deception.

classifications.name: Dreadful Countenance
classifications.description: Whenever you make a Persuasion check or a Use the Force check to activate a Fear effect, you may reroll the check, but the result of the reroll must be accepted, even if it is worse.

classifications.name: Hold Together
classifications.description: As a Reaction, you can spend a Force Point to delay the effect of damage dealt to a Vehicle you are riding in or piloting until the end of the round. The Vehicle must be of Colossal size or smaller.

classifications.name: Wroshyr Rage
classifications.description: Gain Bonus Hit Points when Raging.

classifications.name: Triple Crit Specialist
classifications.description: When you score a critical hit with the selected weapon group, you deal triple damage and move the target -1 step down the condition track.

classifications.name: Shake It Off
classifications.description: You can spend two Swift Actions instead of three to move +1 step along the Condition Track.

classifications.name: Relentless Attack
classifications.description: Choose one weapon group or Exotic Weapon for which you have the Double Attack Feat. Whenever you miss a target with that weapon, you gain a +2 competence bonus on your next attack roll made before the end of your next turn against the missed target.

classifications.name: Signature Device
classifications.description:
You designate a single weapon, suit of armor, vehicle, or other item as your Signature Item. When making Mechanics checks to modify that piece of Equipment, you can Take 10 on the check. In addition, you can allow the device to gain two Traits from the Tech Specialist feat.

To install the second Trait, you must succeed on a DC 30 Mechanics check. Once installed, the device can use only one Trait at a time, and switching from one Trait to the other requires a Swift Action to adjust the item's settings.

You can have only one Signature Device at a time, but you can designate another device as your Signature Item (the former item loses all benefits gained from this Feat).

classifications.name: Spring Attack
classifications.description: When making a melee attack, you may move both before and after the attack, up to your speed, without provoking attacks of opportunity from the target.

classifications.name: Cleave
classifications.description: If you deal an opponent enough damage to reduce its hit points to 0, you get an immediate extra melee attack against another opponent within your reach. The extra attack is with the same weapon and at the same attack bonus as the attack that dropped the previous opponent. You can use this ability once per round.

classifications.name: Binary Mind
classifications.description: Enemies must roll twice, keeping the lower result, on Mind-Affecting effects.

classifications.name: Moving Target
classifications.description: If you end your turn at least 3 squares away from where you started, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Improved Bantha Rush
classifications.description: When making a Bantha Rush, you push your opponent a number of additional squares away from you equal to half your Strength modifier (Round down, minimum 2 squares pushed total).

classifications.name: Double Attack
classifications.description: Choose a single Exotic Weapon or weapon group with which you are proficient. When you use the Full Attack action, you may make one additional attack when wielding such a weapon. However, you take a -5 penalty on all attack rolls until your next turn because you're trading precision for speed.

classifications.name: Justice Seeker
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to damage rolls against those who harm your allies.

classifications.name: Starship Tactics
classifications.description: You add to your Starship Maneuver Suite a number of Starship Maneuvers equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). You can add the same Starship Maneuver more than once.

classifications.name: Bad Feeling
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Broad passive or utility feat; no stronger subsystem signal found in catalog text.
classifications.description: You can always take a Move Action during a Surprise Round, even if you are Surprised. If you are not Surprised, you can take this Move Action in addition to any other Actions you are normally allowed to take in the Surprise Round.

classifications.name: Forceful Saber Throw
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Saber Throw.

classifications.name: Melee Defense
classifications.description: When you use a Standard Action to make a melee attack, you can take a penalty of up to -5 on your attack roll and add the same number (up to +5) as a dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense. This number may not exceed your Base Attack Bonus. The changes to attack rolls and Reflex Defense last until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Gearhead
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you can make Mechanics and Use Computer checks more quickly than normal. A check requiring a Full-Round Action can be attempted as a Standard Action, a check requiring a Standard Action can be attempted as a Move Action, and a check requiring a Move Action can be attempted as a Swift Action. Checks requiring multiple Swift Actions can be reduced by one Swift Action. Checks requiring more than a Full-Round Action can be attempted in one-half the amount of time required at a -10 penalty.

classifications.name: Reactive Stealth
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you may reroll a Stealth check and take the better result.

classifications.name: Triple Crit
classifications.description: Choose one Weapon with which you are proficient. When you score a Critical Hit with the selected weapon, you deal triple damage. You may select " Unarmed Attack " as a Weapon for purposes of this Feat.

classifications.name: Throw
classifications.description: If you successfully Trip an opponent with a Grapple attack, the opponent falls Prone in any unoccupied space you desire up to 1 square beyond your reach and take bludgeoning damage equal to your Unarmed attack damage. A Thrown opponent is no longer considered Grappled.

classifications.name: Power Attack
classifications.description: On your turn, before making an attack roll, you may choose to subtract a number from all melee attack rolls and add the same number to all melee damage rolls. This number may not exceed your Base Attack Bonus. The penalty on attacks and bonus on damage rolls applies until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Reckless Charge
classifications.description: When you charge, you gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls but take a -2 penalty to Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Hasty Modification
classifications.description: You can exchange a Trait you have applied to a piece of Equipment or Droid for another one by spending 1 minute to make a DC 20 Mechanics check. If you succeed, the Equipment loses the old Trait and gains a new Trait until the end of the encounter, at which point the device loses all Traits previously acquired through use of the Tech Specialist Feat.

classifications.name: Acrobatic Strike
classifications.description: If you succeed in Tumbling to avoid an Attack of Opportunity, you gain a +2 competence bonus on the next attack roll you make against that foe as long as the attack occurs before the end of your current turn.

classifications.name: Heavy Weapon Proficiency
classifications.description: You are proficient with heavy weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

classifications.name: Veteran Spacer
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to Use Computer checks made to Astrogate.

classifications.name: Destructive Force
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Name suggests Force, but catalog benefit is vehicle/object damage splash; confirm exact source context before automation.
classifications.description: When you deal damage to an object or Vehicle that equals or exceeds the target's Damage Threshold and reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, you deal 1 die of damage (of the same type as the damage used in the attack) to all targets, including allies, that are adjacent to the Vehicle or object.

classifications.name: Tech Specialist
classifications.description:
You can modify a device, suit of Armor, Weapon, Droid, or Vehicle so that it gains a special trait. Specific traits are given on the table below. You may perform only one modification at a time. Unless noted otherwise, you cannot grant more than one benefit to a single device, suit of Armor, Weapon, Droid, or Vehicle, and you cannot apply the same benefit more than once.

Before beginning the modification, you must pay one-tenth the cost of the device, suit of Armor, Weapon, Droid, or Vehicle you wish to modify or 1,000 credits, whichever is more. Completing the modification requires 1 day per 1,000 credits of the modification's cost. At the end of this time, make a DC 20 Mechanics check; you can't Take 10 or Take 20 on this check. If the check succeeds, the modification is completed successfully, and the object gains the desired trait. If the check fails, you lose all credits spent making the modification, and the object doesn't gain the desired trait. However, you may start over if you wish.

Other characters Trained in the Mechanics Skill can assist you, reducing proportionately the time needed to complete the modification (for example, 3 people working together on a 1,000 credit modification would take 1/3 of one day). At the end of the modification process, they can make a Mechanics check to aid your check.

The market value of a modified item is equal to the base cost of the item + double the cost of the modifications made to it (not including credits wasted on failed modification attempts).

ARMOR TRAIT | BENEFIT
Agile Armor | The Armor's Maximum Dexterity Bonus increases by 1.
Fortifying Armor | The Armor's Equipment bonus to Fortitude Defense increases by 1.
Protective Armor | The Armor's Armor bonus to Reflex Defense increases by 1.
DEVICE TRAIT | BENEFIT
Enhanced Strength | Increase the device's Strength score by 2.
Improved Durability | The device's Damage Reduction increases by 1, and it gains extra Hit Points equal to one-quarter of its maximum Hit Points.
Mastercraft Device | Skill Checks made using the device gain a +1 Equipment bonus, or the device's existing Equipment bonus increases by 1.
DROID TRAIT | BENEFIT
Enhanced Dexterity | Increase the Droid's Dexterity score by 2.
Enhanced Intelligence | Increase the Droid's Intelligence score by 2.
Enhanced Strength | Increase the Droid's Strength score by 2.
VEHICLE TRAIT | BENEFIT
Enhanced Dexterity | Increase the Vehicle's Dexterity score by 2.
Improved Shields | Increase the Vehicle's Shield Rating by 5.
Improved Speed | Increase the Vehicle's Speed by one-quarter of its base Speed (minimum 1 square).
WEAPON TRAIT | BENEFIT
Improved Accuracy | The Weapon gains a +1 Equipment bonus on attack rolls.
Improved Damage | The Weapon deals +2 points of damage with a successful hit. If the weapon has a damage multiplier (for example, 6d10x2), apply the extra damage before applying the multiplier.
Selective Fire | An Autofire -only ranged Weapon can be set to fire Single-Shots, or a Single-Shot Weapon can be made to have an Autofire mode. This cannot be applied to Weapons that have a Burst radius or Splash radius.

classifications.name: Maniacal Charge
classifications.description: When using the Charge Action, you can make a Persuasion check as a Free Action to Intimidate your target and each enemy you pass within 1 square of. When your check succeeds against an enemy you pass, it cannot make an Attack of Opportunity against you. When your check succeeds against your target, it loses its Dexterity bonus and is considered Flat-Footed until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Ion Shielding
classifications.description: If the Ion damage (Before being halved) equals or exceeds your Damage Threshold, you move only -1 step on the Condition Track.

classifications.name: Quick Draw
classifications.description: You can Draw or Holster a Weapon as a Swift Action instead of a Move Action.

classifications.name: Duck and Cover
classifications.description: Whenever you are the target of an Area Attack that misses, once per turn you can Move 2 squares as a Reaction. This movement does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.

classifications.name: Dodge
classifications.description: During your turn, you designate an opponent and receive a +1 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense against attacks from that opponent. You can select a new opponent on any action. A situation that makes you lose your Dexterity bonus to Reflex Defense (if any) also makes you lose dodge bonuses. Also, dodge bonuses stack with each other, unlike most other types of bonuses.

classifications.name: Opportunistic Shooter
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Attacks of Opportunity with ranged weapons.

classifications.name: Forceful Grip
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Force Grip.

classifications.name: Keen Scent
classifications.description: Increase the range of your Scent ability by 20 squares.

classifications.name: Grappling Strike
classifications.description: When you hit with a melee attack, you may initiate a grapple as a free action without provoking an attack of opportunity.

classifications.name: Separatist Military Training
classifications.description: While you are adjacent to at least one of your allies, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on any one attack roll you make on your turn.

classifications.name: Stay Up
classifications.description: Once per encounter, when you would normally take damage from an attack, you can instead choose to take half damage and move -1 step along the Condition Track.

classifications.name: Pinpoint Accuracy
classifications.description: When using the Aiming Accuracy Feat, a target you damage cannot take the Recover Action until the end of its next turn.

classifications.name: Resurgent Vitality
classifications.description: Gain additional Hit Points when catching a Second Wind.

classifications.name: Rapid Assault
classifications.description:
If you are wielding two weapons or a weapon with which you can use the Double Attack feat, you may spend a Force Point to make two attacks as a Standard Action.

The normal penalties due to attacking with two weapons or using Double Attack still apply, and you cannot make more than two attacks regardless of how many extra attacks you can make during a Full Attack Action.

classifications.name: Collateral Damage
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Catalog metadata suggests more than one plausible home; consult source text before using this taxonomy for automation.
classifications.description: When you deal damage with a single, non- Area Attack using the Rapid Shot Feat, once per turn on your turn, you can immediately make a second attack at a -2 penalty against a second target within 2 squares of the first. If this attack hits, you deal half the original attack's damage to that target.

classifications.name: Running Attack
classifications.description: When making an attack with a melee or ranged weapon, you can move both before and after the attack, provided that the total distance moved is not greater than your Speed.

classifications.name: Anointed Hunter
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Broad passive or utility feat; no stronger subsystem signal found in catalog text.
classifications.description: If you end your Move at least 2 squares from where you started, you gain a +1 competence bonus on attacks made with Thrown Weapons until the end of your turn.

classifications.name: Extra Second Wind
classifications.description: You can catch your Second Wind one additional time per day, but you can still only catch a Second Wind once per encounter.

classifications.name: Delay Damage
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you may delay the effects of damage taken until the end of your next turn.

classifications.name: Blaster Barrage
classifications.description: When you make an attack with a weapon set on Autofire that deals damage to at least one target within the designated area, you grant a +2 circumstance bonus on any of your allies' Autofire attacks made against that same target until the beginning of your next turn.

classifications.name: Opportunistic Retreat
classifications.description: Whenever an opponent provokes an Attack of Opportunity from you, once per turn you can sacrifice the attack to move a number of squares equal to one-half your Speed. Moving in this way does not provoke Attacks of Opportunity.

classifications.name: Stand Tall
classifications.description: Once per encounter when you take damage, all allies who are within 6 squares of you and within your line of sight can, as a Reaction, make a single attack on the target that damaged you.

classifications.name: Sith Military Training
classifications.description: Once per encounter, as a Reaction when you reduce a target to 0 Hit Points or deal damage that exceeds the target's Damage Threshold, you can activate this Feat to cause all enemies within 6 squares of that target to take a -2 penalty to all Defenses until the end of your next turn. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

classifications.name: Attack Combo (Fire and Strike)
classifications.description: If you hit a single target with two consecutive ranged, melee, and/or Unarmed attacks during the same turn, any additional ranged, melee, or Unarmed attacks you make until the end of your next turn (including Attacks of Opportunity and attacks made as Reactions) deal +1 die of damage on a hit. This extra damage stacks with extra damage granted by other Feats or Talents.

classifications.name: Force Boon
classifications.description: You gain three additional Force Points at each level.

classifications.name: Unwavering Resolve
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Catalog metadata suggests more than one plausible home; consult source text before using this taxonomy for automation.
classifications.description: You gain a +5 insight bonus to Will Defense against Deception and Persuasion checks.

classifications.name: Headstrong
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Will Defense against fear effects and may reroll a failed Will Defense check once per encounter.

classifications.name: Recurring Success
classifications.description: When you select this Feat, choose one Talent or Feat that can normally be used once per encounter. You can use that Talent or Feat one additional time per encounter.

classifications.name: Aquatic Specialists
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Swim checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Sniper
classifications.description: You always ignore Soft Cover (that is, Cover provided by a character, creature, or Droid) when you make a ranged attack.

classifications.name: Improved Grapple
classifications.description: You gain a +5 bonus on grapple checks and do not provoke attacks of opportunity when initiating a grapple.

classifications.name: Improved Sleight of Hand
classifications.description:
When using the Sleight of Hand application of the Stealth Skill, you can use your Deception Skill to improve your chances of success. Prior to making the Stealth check, you can make a Deception check as a Swift Action. The opposed Perception check of anyone attempting to notice your action must exceed both your Deception check and your Stealth check.

Additionally, you can, as a single Action, use Improved Sleight of Hand to simultaneously draw and palm a weapon two sizes smaller than you. Make a Stealth check as a Free Action when drawing your weapon to successfully conceal it in a position in your hand (Or similar appendage), where you can use it on a following Action. Once the weapon is used, you can attempt to palm it again as a Swift Action.

classifications.name: Leader of Droids
classifications.description: Whenever you provide a beneficial, Mind-Affecting effect to your allies, you may select from among these allies a number of Droids equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). The Droids you designate ignore their Mind-Affecting immunity with regards to this effect, provided they are willing to ignore this immunity.

classifications.name: Perfect Intuition
classifications.description: Reroll Initiative checks, always keeping the better result.

classifications.name: Linguist
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Handled through language selection/progression rather than passive feat math.
classifications.description: You gain a number of additional Languages equal to 1 + your Intelligence modifier (Minimum 1).

classifications.name: Recovering Surge
classifications.description: When you catch a Second Wind, you move +1 step on the Condition Track.

classifications.name: Martial Arts II
classifications.description: Damage dealt by your Unarmed attacks increases by one die step. In addition, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense (Which stacks with the dodge bonus granted by the Martial Arts I Feat).

classifications.name: Wicked Strike
classifications.description: When you damage a target with a single, non- Area Attack using the Rapid Strike Feat, once per turn on your turn, you can immediately make a second attack at a -2 penalty against a second target within your reach. If this attack hits, you deal half of the original attack's damage to that target.

classifications.name: Droid Hunter
classifications.description: While wielding a weapon with which you are proficient, you gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls against Droid enemies, or +4 if you are using a weapon that deals Ion Damage.

classifications.name: Experienced Medic
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Catalog metadata suggests more than one plausible home; consult source text before using this taxonomy for automation.
classifications.description: You can perform Surgery on a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 2) simultaneously. You make Treat Injury checks for each individual creature as normal.

classifications.name: Vehicular Surge
classifications.description: If the Vehicle you Pilot is Colossal or smaller and is reduced to fewer than one-half its maximum Hit Points, once per day as a Swift Action your Vehicle gains a number of Bonus Hit Points equal to one-quarter its maximum Hit Point total. Damage is subtracted from Bonus Hit Points first, and any Bonus Hit Points remaining at the end of the encounter are lost. Bonus Hit Points do not stack.

classifications.name: Attack Combo (Melee)
classifications.description: If you hit a single target with two consecutive melee and/or Unarmed attacks during the same turn, any additional melee or Unarmed attacks you make until the end of your next turn (including Attacks of Opportunity and attacks made as Reactions) deal +1 die of damage on a hit. This extra damage stacks with extra damage granted by other Feats or Talents.

classifications.name: Whirlwind Attack
classifications.description: As a Full-Round Action, you can make an Area Attack with your melee weapon, striking every target within your reach. This Whirlwind Attack uses the Area Attack rules; you make one attack roll and apply the result to every target in range.

classifications.name: Nikto Survival
classifications.description: Select a Nikto subspecies: Kajain'sa'Nikto (Desert), Kadas'sa'Nikto (Forest), Esral'sa'Nikto (Mountains), Gluss'sa'Nikto (Ocean), M'shento'su,Nikto (Arctic). In addition to the usual Nikto Species Traits, when operating in an environment advantageous to your native subspecies, you can reroll any Survival check, taking the better result.

classifications.name: Knife Trick
classifications.description:
If you have a concealed weapon, you Threaten squares as though armed with a melee weapon. When you can make an Attack of Opportunity, you can draw a weapon you have successfully concealed on your person and make a single attack against the target.

If you choose not to draw your concealed weapon and attack with it, you cannot make the Attack of Opportunity unless you would otherwise normally be able to do so.

classifications.name: Forceful Recovery
classifications.description: Whenever you catch a Second Wind, choose one expended Force Power and return that power to your Force Power Suite.

classifications.name: Careful Shot
classifications.description: If you Aim before making a ranged attack, you gain a +1 bonus on your attack roll.

classifications.name: Flood of Fire
classifications.description: When you make an Area Attack with a weapon set on Autofire, and you are proficient with that weapon, all targets in the area lose all dodge or deflection bonuses to Reflex Defense against the Autofire attack.

classifications.name: Turn and Burn
classifications.description: When using the Withdraw Action, you can move up to 2 squares to escape Threatened squares without provoking an Attack of Opportunity, and you can move your Speed. You can spend a Force Point as a Reaction to an enemy ending its movement adjacent to you to Withdraw.

classifications.name: Impulsive Flight
classifications.description: You can Withdraw one extra square using the Withdraw Action.

classifications.name: Resolute Stance
classifications.description: When you Fight Defensively, you gain a +2 morale bonus to your Will Defense. If you make no attacks until your next turn, you gain a +5 morale bonus to your Will Defense until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Swarm
classifications.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on melee attack rolls for each allied character adjacent to your target.

classifications.name: Keen Force Mind
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate mind-affecting Force powers.

classifications.name: Triple Attack
classifications.description: Choose a single Exotic Weapon or weapon group. When you use the Full Attack Action, you may make one additional attack when wielding such a Weapon. However, you take a -5 penalty on all attack rolls until your next turn because you're trading precision for speed. The extra attack and penalty stack with those of Double Attack.

classifications.name: Distracting Droid
classifications.description: As a Standard Action, make a Persuasion check against the Will Defense of all enemies that are within 6 squares of you and that can see or hear you, as determined by the Gamemaster. If the check equals or exceeds an enemy's Will Defense, the enemy loses one Move Action on its next turn. If the attack exceeds an enemy's Will Defense by 10 or more, the enemy is also Flat-Footed until the start of your next turn. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

classifications.name: Forceful Slam
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Force Slam.

classifications.name: Mandalorian Training
classifications.description: When using the Charging Fire Feat, you gain a +2 bonus to a ranged attack made at the end of a Charge. As with a normal Charge, you still take a -2 penalty to your Reflex Defense, but you also gain a +2 morale bonus to your Will Defense until the beginning of your next turn.

classifications.name: Demoralizing Strike
classifications.description: When you successfully deal damage to an opponent with an Attack of Opportunity, you can immediately make a Persuasion check to Intimidate the opponent as a Free Action.

classifications.name: Tae-Jitsu Training
classifications.description: Deal more damage on critical hits. Use Dodge against enemies.

classifications.name: Elder's Knowledge
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you can substitute a Knowledge (Galactic Lore) or Knowledge (Social Sciences) check for a Wisdom or Wisdom -related check.

classifications.name: Scavenger
classifications.description: You can spend 1 hour scavenging materials from other Vehicles or objects. When you do so, you make a Perception check to determine the value of the parts scavenged. You produce raw materials equal in value to the result of your Perception check x 30 credits. You must apply these raw materials toward the cost of construction of a single object (see Build Object), and you can scavenge parts for only a single object at any given time. Additionally, you can scavenge raw materials only once for any given object you are attempting to build.

classifications.name: Trustworthy
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Persuasion checks and may reroll a failed Persuasion check once per encounter.

classifications.name: Hijkata Training
classifications.description: Make counterattacks. Impose penalties to enemy attacks.

classifications.name: Crossfire
classifications.description: If you miss when making a ranged attack against a target that has Soft Cover (that is, Cover provided by another character, creature, or Droid), you can immediately make an attack roll (with the same weapon and at the same attack bonus) against the target that is providing the Soft Cover. You can only use this Feat once per round.

classifications.name: Deadeye
classifications.description: If you Aim before making a ranged attack and the attack hits, increase the damage you deal by an additional weapon die (3d6 becomes 4d6).

classifications.name: Risk Taker
classifications.description: You fall only if you fail a Climb check by 10 or more. Additionally, if you make a Jump check and fail to land on a safe or solid surface (such as if you fall into a pit or fail to clear a stream of lava), you can spend a Force Point as a Free Action and add the result of the Force Point roll to your distance jumped. However, you must land in the first available safe square.

classifications.name: Deadly Sniper
classifications.description: When you make a ranged attack against a target that is unaware of you, you gain a +2 bonus on your attack roll and deal +1 die of damage on the first attack each turn.

classifications.name: Gunnery Specialist
classifications.description: While you are the Gunner of a Vehicle, you are considered proficient with Vehicle Weapons. Additionally, once per encounter, you can reroll an attack roll made with a Vehicle Weapon, though you must keep the second result, even if it is worse. You can declare this reroll after you learn the result of the attack roll, but before damage is resolved.

classifications.name: Perfect Swimmer
classifications.description: Reroll Swim checks, always keeping the better result.

classifications.name: Instinctive Attack
classifications.description: When you use a Force Point when making an attack with a weapon with which you are proficient, you can reroll the attack and take the better result. Apply the results of the die from the Force Point to the better result.

classifications.name: Combat Reflexes
classifications.description:
When opponents leave themselves open, you may make a number of additional Attacks of Opportunity equal to your Dexterity modifier.

With this Feat, you may also make Attacks of Opportunity while Flat-Footed. You can still only make one attack for each triggered Attack of Opportunity.

classifications.name: Strong in the Force
classifications.description: When you spend a Force Point to adjust the result of an attack roll, Skill Check, or Ability Check, you roll d8s instead of d6s.

classifications.name: Grand Army of the Republic Training
classifications.description: If you wear Armor that provides an Equipment bonus to your Fortitude Defense, and you are proficient with that Armor, you also apply the Armor's Equipment bonus to your Will Defense.

classifications.name: Unhindered Approach
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Jump checks checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Acrobatic Ally
classifications.description: You can hoist or toss an ally of your size or smaller up to 2 squares. You and your ally must act on the same Initiative Count and start in adjacent squares. If you succeed on a DC 20 Acrobatics check as a Standard Action, your ally ends up in a square of your choosing up to 2 squares above or away from you. This counts as a Free Action for your ally and ends your turn. Your ally can then continue his or her turn. If you fail your check, both you and your ally immediately become Prone in your square. Your turn is ended, but your ally can take his or her turn normally.

classifications.name: Returning Bug
classifications.description: When you miss with a Razor Bug or a Thud Bug, the weapon returns to your hand immediately.

classifications.name: Nimble Team
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Acrobatics checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Deceptive Drop
Covert Operatives
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Stealth checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Weapon Proficiency (Rifles)
classifications.description: You are proficient with rifles and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

classifications.name: Armor Proficiency (light)
classifications.description: When you wear Light Armor, you take no Armor Check Penalty on attack rolls or Skill Checks. Additionally, you benefit from all of the armor's special Equipment bonuses (if any).

classifications.name: Slicer Team
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Use Computer checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Biotech Surgery
classifications.description: You can install Bio-Implants onto a living being. The surgical procedure takes 1 hour of uninterrupted work, after which you must make a DC 20 Treat Injury check. If the check succeeds, the prosthesis is installed correctly. If the check fails, the prosthesis is not installed properly; however, you can try again after another uninterrupted hour of surgery.

classifications.name: Crush
classifications.description: If you successfully Pin an opponent with a Grapple attack (see Pin Feat for more details), you can immediately deal bludgeoning damage to it equal to your Unarmed damage or claw damage, whichever is greater.

classifications.name: Trench Warrior
classifications.description: When you are adjacent to a wall or object that provides you with Cover from your target's ranged attacks, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on your attack rolls against that target.

classifications.name: Imperial Military Training
classifications.description: Once per encounter, as a Free Action on your turn, you can negate any one Mind-Affecting effect targeting or currently affecting you.

classifications.name: Overwhelming Attack
classifications.description: You must spend two Swift Actions in the same round to activate this Feat. If your target attempts to use a Talent, Feat, or other ability to negate your attack (Such as Block, Deflect, or Vehicular Combat), the target takes a -5 penalty to any attack rolls or Skill Checks it makes to negate the attack. This effect applies to the next attack you make before the end of the same round.

classifications.name: Aiming Accuracy
classifications.description: If you Aim at a target as a Full-Round Action (instead of two Swift Actions), you gain a +5 bonus to your next attack made with a weapon with which you are proficient in the following round against that target. The target must remain in your line of sight.

classifications.name: Return Fire
classifications.description: Select one Exotic Weapon or Weapon Group for which you possess the Weapon Focus feat. Once per encounter as a Reaction, you can make a single ranged attack with the chosen weapon against an enemy that misses you with a ranged attack, provided you have line of sight to the enemy.

classifications.name: Multi-Grab
classifications.description: As a Standard Action, you may make a Grab attack against two targets adjacent to you (roll a separate attack against each target). You must have two empty hands to use this Feat.

classifications.name: Weapon Proficiency (Heavy Weapons)
classifications.description: You are proficient with heavy weapons and do not take the -5 penalty when using them.

classifications.name: Stava Training
classifications.description: Grab and Grapple as a larger creature. Grab after Charging.

classifications.name: Intimidating Presence
classifications.description: You may use Persuasion to intimidate as a swift action instead of a standard action.

classifications.name: Dual Weapon Mastery I
classifications.description: When you attack with two weapons, or with both ends of a double weapon as part of a Full Attack Action, you take a -5 penalty (Instead of a -10 penalty) on all attack rolls until the start of your next turn. You only gain this reduced penalty if you are wielding a weapon with which you are proficient.

classifications.name: Forceful Telekinesis
classifications.description: You may spend a Force Point to move a target -1 step down the condition track when using Move Object.

classifications.name: Armor Proficiency (heavy)
classifications.description: When you wear Heavy Armor, you take no Armor Check Penalty on attack rolls or Skill Checks. Additionally, you benefit from all of the armor's special Equipment bonuses (if any).

classifications.name: Heroic Surge
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you may take an additional standard or move action on your turn.

classifications.name: Sport Hunter
classifications.description:
When you make a ranged attack with a slugthrower or sporting weapon with which you are proficient, you gain one of the following benefits, depending on the kind of weapon you use.

- Slugthrower Pistol: When you use a Slugthrower Pistol at Point-Blank Range, you deal +1 die of damage with the weapon.
- Slugthrower Rifle: When you use a Slugthrower Rifle, the damage dice increase from d8 to d12.
- Sporting Blaster Pistol: When you use a Sporting Blaster Pistol, reroll any result of 1 on the damage dice until you get a result other than 1.
- Sporting Blaster Rifle: When using a Sporting Blaster Rifle, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls when you Aim before firing.

classifications.name: Flawless Pilot
classifications.description: Reroll Pilot checks, always keeping the better result.

classifications.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
classifications.description: You reduce the penalty for fighting with two weapons by 2.

classifications.name: Halt
classifications.description:
If you hit with an Attack of Opportunity, while wielding a weapon for which you possess the Weapon Focus Feat, against an enemy of up to one size larger than you, compare the same die roll against the target's Grapple check. If the die roll succeeds against the Grapple check, the target immediately halts all movement and falls Prone in its current square. The target can spend any remaining Actions normally.

If your attack exceeds the target's Damage Threshold, the target loses any remaining Actions and ends its turn immediately. Apply damage normally to the target regardless of the results of this Feat.

classifications.name: Great Cleave
classifications.description: As the Cleave Feat, except that you have no limit to the number of times you can use it per round.

classifications.name: Force Readiness
classifications.description: You can spend Force Points as a Free Action, even if it is not your turn. All other restrictions to Force Point use still apply.

classifications.name: Never Surrender
classifications.description: The first time you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points in an encounter, you can make an Endurance check as a Reaction, with a DC equal to the number of points of damage being dealt to you. If your check succeeds, you are instead reduced to 1 Hit Point.

classifications.name: Steadying Position
classifications.description: When you are Prone and Aim before making a ranged attack, your target does not benefit from its Dexterity bonus to its Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Frightening Cleave
classifications.description: Immediately after you use the Cleave Feat, each enemy within 6 squares of you and within your line of sight takes a -1 penalty to its Reflex Defense, attack rolls, and Skill Checks against you until the end of the encounter. This penalty stacks with itself to a maximum of -5. This is a Mind-Affecting effect.

classifications.name: Desperate Gambit
classifications.description: Once per turn, when you miss on an attack roll, you reroll the attack, but you take a -2 penalty to Reflex Defense until the end of your next turn. If your first attack missed because you rolled a Natural 1, you can still use this Feat, but you take a -5 penalty to Reflex Defense instead. You must accept the result of the second roll, even if it's worse than the first.

classifications.name: Opportunistic Trickery
classifications.description: Whenever an opponent provokes an Attack of Opportunity from you, once per turn you can sacrifice that attack to reduce the target's Reflex Defense by 2 for the following round.

classifications.name: Pall of the Dark Side
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Catalog metadata suggests more than one plausible home; consult source text before using this taxonomy for automation.
classifications.description: You can add one-half your Dark Side Score (Minimum 1) to any Use the Force checks made to resist attempts to detect you with the Sense Force application of the Use the Force skill.

classifications.name: Cunning Attack
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls against a Flat-Footed enemy or one who is denied its Dexterity bonus to its Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Silver Tongue
classifications.description: You can Intimidate or Change Attitude as a Standard Action.

classifications.name: Survivor of Ryloth
classifications.description: Make Survival checks to resist Extreme Heat and Extreme Cold.

classifications.name: Improved Knock Prone
classifications.description: You gain a +5 bonus on attack rolls made to knock an opponent prone.

classifications.name: Dreadful Rage
classifications.description: While Raging, your Rage bonus on melee attack rolls and melee damage rolls increases to +5.

classifications.name: Martial Arts I
classifications.description: Damage dealt by your Unarmed Attacks increases by one die step. In addition, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense. Additionally, you do not provoke Attacks of Opportunity with Unarmed Attacks.

classifications.name: Rancor Crush
classifications.description: When you successfully Pin an enemy with the Pin Feat and use the Crush Feat at the same time, the enemy moves -1 step down the Condition Track in addition to taking damage.

classifications.name: Power Blast
classifications.description: As a Swift Action, before making an attack roll, you can choose to subtract a number from all ranged attack rolls and add the same number to all ranged damage rolls. This number cannot exceed your Base Attack Bonus. The penalty on attacks and bonus on damage applies until the start of your next turn but you gain no bonus to damage with Area Attacks or when attacking objects or Vehicles with this Feat.

classifications.name: Disabler
classifications.description:
When making a ranged attack with an Ion Weapon with which you are proficient, you gain one of the following benefits, depending on the kind of weapon you use.

- Ion Grenade: When you use an Ion Grenade, the Grenade's Burst radius is 3 squares instead of 2 squares.
- Ion Pistol: When you use an Ion Pistol, the damage dice increase from d6 to d8.
- Ion Rifle: You treat the Ion Rifle as an Accurate Weapon.

classifications.name: Rapid Shot
classifications.description: When using a ranged weapon with which you are proficient, you may fire two shots as a single attack against a single target. You take a -2 penalty on your attack roll, but you deal +1 die of damage with a successful attack.

classifications.name: Sniper Shot
classifications.description: When wielding only ranged weapons with which you are proficient, you gain a +2 bonus on all your ranged attacks until the beginning of your next turn, but you take a -5 penalty to Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Quick Skill
classifications.description: Once per encounter, you can Take 10 when rushed on a single Skill Check for a Skill you are Trained in, unless the Skill description explicitly forbids it. Alternatively, you can Take 20 when using a Skill you are Trained in at half the normal time required.

classifications.name: Disarming Charm
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to Skill Checks on targets whose Attitude you have improved.

classifications.name: Coordinated Attack
classifications.description: You are automatically successful when using the Aid Another action to aid an ally's attack, or suppress an enemy as long as the target is adjacent to you or within Point-Blank Range.

classifications.name: Improved Damage Threshold
classifications.description: You increase your Damage Threshold by 5 points.

classifications.name: Fast Talk
classifications.description: You may make a Persuasion check to feint in combat as a swift action instead of a standard action.

classifications.name: Martial Arts III
classifications.description: Damage dealt by your Unarmed attacks increases by one die step. In addition, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense (Which stacks with the dodge bonuses granted by the Martial Arts I and Martial Arts II Feats).

classifications.name: Bantha Herder
classifications.description:
When you damage a Large or smaller creature with a ranged attack with a weapon you are proficient with, compare the result of your attack roll to the target's Will Defense. If your result equals or exceeds the target's Will Defense, you can move the target 1 square in any direction as a Free Action.

If your ranged attack deals damage to multiple eligible creatures, you can use this benefit against all of them. You can't move a target that's being Grabbed or Grappled, and you can't move a target into a solid object or into another creature's Fighting Space.

classifications.name: Mind of Reason
classifications.description: Use Wisdom instead of Intelligence for Intelligence-based Skill Checks.

classifications.name: Unswerving Resolve
classifications.description: Whenever you are targeted by a Fear effect or Mind-Affecting effect that fails to affect you, you gain a temporary Force Point. If this Force Point is not used before the end of your next turn, it goes away. If you negate the contingent effect in any way, you do not gain the benefit of this Feat.

classifications.name: Mighty Throw
classifications.description: You can add your Strength modifier (in addition to your Dexterity modifier) to your ranged attack bonus when using Thrown Weapons (including Grenades and grenadelike weapons). Also, you can increase the length of each Range category by a number of squares equal to your Strength modifier.

classifications.name: Erratic Target
classifications.description: You can reduce your Speed by up to 2 squares to increase your Dodge bonus by 1 for each square of speed you give up, until the start of your next turn. You must move at least 2 squares to gain this benefit.

classifications.name: Great Fortitude
classifications.description: You gain a +2 bonus to your Fortitude Defense.

classifications.name: Combat Trickery
classifications.description: You can spend two successive Swift Actions on the same turn to make a Deception check against a target's Will Defense. If the check is successful, the target is Flat-Footed against your next attack made before the end of your next turn. Additionally, you can spend a Force Point to extend your target's penalties until the end of encounter.

classifications.name: Feat of Strength
classifications.description: Once per encounter as a Full-Round Action, you can Take 20 on a Strength check or Strength -based Skill Check (You must be Trained in that Skill), even if distracted or threatened.

classifications.name: Force Training
classifications.description: You add to your Force Power Suite a number of Force Powers equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (Minimum 1). You can add the same Force Power more than once.

classifications.name: Targeted Area
classifications.description: When you make a successful Area Attack with a weapon with which you are proficient, select a target hit by the attack. That target takes an additional 5 points of damage from the attack, before applying the effect of the Evasion Talent.

classifications.name: Slammer
classifications.description: As a Standard Action, you slam two of your Appendages together from either side of an enemy, crushing your target between them. Make a melee attack. If the attack is successful, you deal Unarmed damage with double your Strength bonus. If you also exceed the target's Damage Threshold, it has a Persistent Condition that can only be removed with 8 hours of rest or a DC 20 Treat Injury check.

classifications.name: Inborn Resilience
classifications.description: Reduce one Defense bonus but increase another.

classifications.name: Advantageous Attack
classifications.description: When you make a successful attack against an enemy who has not yet acted in combat, you add your full Heroic Level to damage rolls.

classifications.name: Devastating Bellow
classifications.description: Deal more damage with your Bellow attacks.

classifications.name: Vitality Surge
classifications.description: You can catch a Second Wind even if you are not at or below half your maximum Hit Points.

classifications.name: Flash and Clear
classifications.description: When you damage a target with a Burst or Splash weapon, you gain Concealment against that target until the beginning of your next turn.

classifications.name: Heavy Hitter
classifications.description: When making an attack roll using a Weapon Emplacement or a Weapon System, you add an additional 1 point of damage for every 5 points you roll above the target's Reflex Defense. If you exceed the target's Damage Threshold, then on its next turn the target cannot attack and its speed is reduced by 2 squares (1 square Starship Scale).

classifications.name: Rapport
classifications.description: When using the Aid Another Action, you grant an additional +2 insight bonus on Skill Checks and attack rolls to the character you are assisting. This bonus does not stack with any bonus provided by the Noble's Coordinate Talent.

classifications.name: Fight Through Pain
classifications.description: You can use your Will Defense instead of your Fortitude Defense when determining your Damage Threshold.

classifications.name: Resilient Strength
classifications.description: You can use either your Strength modifier or your Constitution modifier to determine your Fortitude Defense.

classifications.name: Expert Briber
classifications.description: When using the Haggle or Bribery applications of the Persuasion skill, you reduce the DC to reduce the price of the item haggled over by 10.

classifications.name: Superior Tech
classifications.description:
Select one of the following: Armor, Weapons, Droids, Vehicles, or Devices. You can install advanced Traits when modifying the selected type of equipment. These Traits replace the Traits granted by the Tech Specialist Feat. You may still use the Tech Specialist options if you so choose.

You must pay one-fifth the cost of the Device, Armor, Weapon, Droid, or vehicle you wish to modify or 2,000 credits, whichever is more, before beginning the modification. This fee replaces the normal cost of modification from the Tech Specialist Feat. Completing the modification requires 1 day per 1,000 credits of the modification's cost. At the end of this time, make a DC 30 Mechanics check; you cannot Take 10 or Take 20 on this check. If the check succeeds, the modification is completed successfully, and the object gains the desired Trait. If the check fails, you lose all credits spent on making the modification, and the object does not gain the desired Trait. However, you can start over from scratch if you wish.

Only characters with the Tech Specialist Feat can assist you, reducing proportionately the time needed to complete the modification. At the end of the modification process, they can make a Mechanics check to aid your check.

The market value of a modified item is equal to the base cost of the item plus double the cost of the modifications made to it (not including credits wasted on failed modification attempts).

classifications.name: Logic Upgrade: Tactician
classifications.description: Grant a +5 bonus to attack with a successful Aid Another attempt.

classifications.name: Trip
classifications.description: If you succeed on a Grappling attack and your opponent fails the opposed Grapple check, it falls Prone in its space and is no longer considered Grappled. A Prone opponent takes a -5 penalty on melee attack rolls. Melee attacks made against a Prone target gain a +5 bonus, while ranged attack made against a prone target take a -5 penalty.

classifications.name: Charging Fire
classifications.description: When you Charge, you may make a ranged attack instead of a melee attack at the end of your movement. Unlike a normal Charge, your momentum does not help you overcome your target, so you gain no bonus on your attack roll. As with a normal Charge, you still take a -2 penalty to your Reflex Defense.

classifications.name: Warrior Heritage
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Catalog metadata suggests more than one plausible home; consult source text before using this taxonomy for automation.
classifications.description: Gain a bonus to Will Defense when using an Atlatl or Cesta.

classifications.name: Skill Training
classifications.description: Choose one Untrained Skill from your list of Class Skills. You become Trained in that Skill.

classifications.name: Savage Attack
classifications.description: Choose one weapon groups or Exotic Weapon you have selected for the Double Attack Feat. When you use a Full Attack Action and successfully hit the target with your first attack, you deal +1 die of damage on each successful attack remaining in the Full Attack made against that target.

classifications.name: Mounted Defense
classifications.description: When riding a Beast as a Mount or on a Speeder Bike (as a passenger or a Pilot), once per encounter you can redirect any attack made against you at your Mount or Vehicle instead. The decision to redirect the attack is made after the result of the attack roll is determined but before damage or other effects are resolved.

classifications.name: Sadistic Strike
classifications.description: When you deliver a Coup de Grace to a Helpless creature, all opponents within line of sight move -1 step on the Condition Track until the end of encounter.

classifications.name: Lasting Influence
classifications.description: After a successful Persuasion check, gain a bonus to future Persuasion checks.

classifications.name: Assured Attack
classifications.description: Whenever you deal damage to a target with an attack and you roll multiple damage dice, you can reroll the lowest damage die but must keep the second result, even if it is worse.

classifications.name: Droidcraft
classifications.description: You can use the Repair Droid aspect of the Mechanics skill in 10 minutes.

classifications.name: Intuitive Initiative
classifications.description: You may reroll your Initiative check and take the better result. You must keep the new result even if it is worse.

classifications.name: Mounted Combat
classifications.description:
You can make a DC 20 Ride check as a Swift Action to spur your living Mount to move faster than its normal speed. If the check fails, your Mount's Speed does not increase and it moves -1 step on the Condition Track. If the check succeeds, your Mount's Speed increases by 2 squares until the start of your next turn. You cannot Take 10 on this check. Your Mount must succeed on a DC 20 Endurance check prior to each subsequent attempt to use this Feat in the same encounter.

Additionally, once per round, as a Reaction, when you are riding a Mount, you can negate a weapon hit against either you or the Mount by making a successful Ride check. The DC of the Skill Check is equal to the result of the attack roll you want to negate.

classifications.name: K'thri Training
classifications.description: Make swift Unarmed attacks. Deal half damage on misses.

classifications.name: Teräs Käsi Training
classifications.description: Treat target's Damage Threshold as lower.

classifications.name: Fringe Benefits
classifications.description: Reduce the cost multiplier of goods bought on the Black Market.

classifications.name: Unified Squadron
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Pilot checks checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Frightful Presence
classifications.description: As a standard action, you may make a Persuasion check to intimidate all enemies within 6 squares. On a success, targets move -1 step down the condition track.

classifications.name: Instinctive Defense
classifications.description: On your turn, as a Free Action, you can spend a Force Point to increase all of your Defenses by 2 until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Cybernetic Surgery
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Cybernetic installation/removal is a medical procedure and remains GM/player handled.
classifications.description: You can install Cybernetic Devices on living beings. The surgical procedure takes 1 hour of uninterrupted work, after which you must make a DC 20 Treat Injury check. If the check succeeds, the Cybernetic Device is installed correctly. If the check fails, the device is not properly installed; however, you can try again after another uninterrupted hour of surgery.

classifications.name: Suppression Fire
classifications.description: When you use the Aid Another action to impose a penalty to an enemy's attack rolls and your attack roll exceeds the target's Will Defense, that enemy must end its next turn in a position where it has Cover from you if possible. Targets whose level is equal to or higher than your Character Level are immune to the effect of Suppression Fire. This is a Mind-Affecting Fear effect.

classifications.name: A Few Maneuvers
classifications.description: When piloting a Vehicle of Colossal size or smaller, you grant a +2 dodge bonus to your Vehicle's Reflex Defense. Furthermore, if you are the target of a missile or torpedo attack and the attack roll misses by 5 or more, the projectile self-destructs harmlessly.

classifications.name: Rebel Military Training
classifications.description: Whenever you use the Running Attack feat to move both before and after making an attack, you gain a +2 dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Master of Disguise
classifications.description: You gain a +5 insight bonus to your Deception check when creating a Deceptive Appearance. In addition, you can rush the process by taking a -2 penalty to the Deception check.

classifications.name: Attack Combo (Ranged)
classifications.description: If you hit a single target with two consecutive ranged attacks during the same turn, any additional ranged attacks you make until the end of your next turn (including Attacks of Opportunity and attacks made as Reactions) deal +1 die of damage on a hit. This extra damage stacks with extra damage granted by other Feats or Talents.

classifications.name: Far Shot
classifications.description: When you use a ranged weapon against targets at Short, Medium, or Long Range, the Range category is considered one less. In other words, you take no penalty on ranged attack rolls made at Short Range, a -2 penalty on ranged attack rolls made against targets at Medium Range, and a -5 penalty on ranged attack rolls made against targets at Long Range.

classifications.name: Long Haft Strike
classifications.description: When you use a Lightsaber Pike or a Long-Handle Lightsaber, you can attack with both ends of the weapon, treating it as a double weapon.

classifications.name: Tireless Squad
classifications.description: Gain bonuses on Endurance checks when near allies.

classifications.name: Improvised Weapon Mastery
classifications.description: You treat all Improvised Weapons as Simple Weapons, and you can use them without penalty and with Talents and Feats designed for use with Simple Weapons. You also deal an additional 1d6 points of damage on a successful hit.

classifications.name: Hew
classifications.description: When you use Power Attack, you ignore an amount of damage reduction equal to your base attack bonus.

classifications.name: Mission Specialist
classifications.description: Choose one Skill in which you are Trained. Allies who are not Trained in that Skill gain a +2 competence bonus to the Skill while they are within 12 squares of you.

classifications.name: Mechanical Martial Arts
classifications.description: When you successfully damage an enemy with an Unarmed Attack, the enemy takes a -5 penalty to all melee attack rolls and damage rolls until the start of your next turn.

classifications.name: Force of Personality
classifications.sourceReviewReason: Name contains Force, but benefit changes Will Defense ability basis; confirm source context before automation.
classifications.description: You can use either your Wisdom modifier or your Charisma modifier to determine your Will Defense.
